meta.changelog: v2.0 — Enriched with research calibration (US influencer market analysis 2026). Added: qCPE as 4th scoring axis, psychology layer (parasocial theory), ROI benchmarks by format, FTC compliance requirement, hybrid compensation model for Gold tier, real candidate examples from kone.vc.

market_context.note: Used for model calibration. Key figures grounding our system design decisions.

format_roi_ranking.relevance_to_horizons: Gold tier target — our system is designed to identify and develop these
Active format. Minimal risk — we pay only for confirmed bookings.
Primary format. Builds content library. High longevity value.
Not used at this stage. Transactional, low parasocial connection.

tourism_case_studies.strategy: Pool of diverse creators showing the city from their own angle — no rigid script
tourism_case_studies.lesson_for_horizons: Our story frames replace the rigid script. Creators get narrative entry point, not a brief. Same principle.

tourism_case_studies.strategy: Niche segmentation — separate creators per audience type (foodies, accessible travel, adventure)
tourism_case_studies.result: 7% growth in tourist spending. 770K impressions on accessible travel content alone.
tourism_case_studies.lesson_for_horizons: Our campaign scenarios (couples, family, wellness etc.) mirror this segmentation logic exactly.

tourism_case_studies.strategy: Integrated YouTube creator into a real user experience moment (app milestone)
tourism_case_studies.lesson_for_horizons: Authenticity of real experience > produced ad. Our stay-first model creates this naturally.

tourism_case_studies.strategy: Local food creator as series host — authority + local trust
tourism_case_studies.result: Poppy Award win + direct revenue lift for local restaurants
tourism_case_studies.lesson_for_horizons: Zone A local creators (Charlotte, SC, NC) carry the same trust multiplier. Prioritise them.

psychology_layer.note: Psychological mechanisms that explain why our gamified, frame-first approach works. Not decorative theory — directly informs how we brief and score influencers.

mechanisms.label: Parasocial connection
mechanisms.description: Followers develop emotional closeness to creators through regular content exposure. The creator feels like a trusted friend, not a celebrity. This is why micro/nano creators convert better than macro — proximity of perception.
mechanisms.horizons_application: Story frames are written from first person. Creator is not advertising — they are experiencing. The audience follows the experience, not the ad.

mechanisms.label: Self-disclosure
mechanisms.description: Creators who share vulnerability (not just wins) are perceived as authentic. Content built on personal emotional response performs better long-term.
mechanisms.horizons_application: Our frames ask 'how would you feel if...' — not 'show our location'. Emotional response is the content. This is the mechanism.

mechanisms.label: Homophily (audience mirror effect)
mechanisms.description: Audiences trust creators who resemble them — same geography, values, lifestyle. A Charlotte local recommending Patrick SC carries more weight than a national travel account.
mechanisms.horizons_application: Zone A scoring weight (35 pts) reflects this. Local is not a compromise — it is a strategic advantage.

mechanisms.label: Social proof + FOMO
mechanisms.description: Promo codes create exclusivity and urgency. Seeing engagement (saves, comments) around a post reinforces the signal. Both activate simultaneously with affiliate model.
mechanisms.horizons_application: Affiliate promo code is not just tracking — it is a psychological trigger. Personalised code per influencer amplifies this (e.g. 'EMILY15' vs generic 'SAVE15').

mechanisms.label: Platform-specific trust mechanics
mechanisms.description: Instagram activates authenticity and parasocial closeness. Facebook activates source authority and argumentation. Our primary platform (Instagram) is optimal for emotional experience content.
mechanisms.horizons_application: Instagram-first approach is correct. Story format (ephemeral, personal) is higher parasocial signal than feed posts.

geo_zones.description: Geographic classification used for candidate scoring. Based on drive distance and audience proximity to Patrick, SC.

A.label: Primary market
A.rationale: Homophily effect is strongest here. Local creators speak to the exact audience within 2hr drive of the property. See tourism case studies: Visit Long Beach success driven by local authority.

B.label: Regional market
B.rationale: Southeast regional audience — reachable destination, relevant lifestyle overlap.

C.label: National / niche
C.rationale: Location-agnostic glamping/travel niche. Acceptable only if ER and content quality are exceptional. Does not activate homophily advantage.

scoring.description: Auto-scoring applied at CSV import. Max score: 100. Prioritises Zone A micro/nano creators with high ER and relevant content.

axes.label: Geo zone fit
axes.research_backing: Homophily principle. Local trust multiplier confirmed by Visit Long Beach case.

axes.label: Engagement rate
axes.research_backing: Micro/nano ER up to 10%+ vs macro 1–2%. ER is proxy for parasocial connection strength.

axes.label: Follower count
axes.research_backing: Micro ($50–$1,000/post) enables diversified pool over single macro. Visit Salt Lake used diverse pool of smaller creators vs one big star.

axes.label: Content / keyword relevance
axes.research_backing: Niche segmentation drives superior ROI (Visit California). Keyword match is fast proxy for content niche alignment.

iteration_scoring.description: Post-campaign scoring per iteration. Tier is computed from cumulative rolling average across all iterations.

axes.label: Content quality
axes.description: Visual quality, narrative depth, format compliance, originality, emotional resonance.
axes.research_backing: Self-disclosure principle: content that reveals genuine emotion outperforms scripted content long-term.

axes.label: Our value received
axes.description: Reach, saves, shares, direct traffic, booking inquiries attributable to this influencer.
axes.research_backing: Direct ROI signal. Affiliate promo code enables clean attribution.

axes.label: Content longevity
axes.description: Is the content reusable for future campaigns, ads, organic posts? Does it hold value over time?
axes.research_backing: Visit Salt Lake key lesson: library of authentic content > one expensive video. Long-tail value is the differentiator.

axes.label: Qualified engagement (qCPE)
axes.description: Quality of engagement actions: saves, meaningful comments (not just emojis), link clicks, DM inquiries, story poll responses. NOT raw likes or reach.
axes.research_backing: Market is shifting from reach to qCPE — qualified cost per engagement. Saves and DM inquiries are highest-intent signals in tourism context.
what_to_measure: Post saves (high intent — user wants to revisit)
Meaningful comments (questions about location, pricing, dates)
Link-in-bio clicks during and after post

iteration_scoring.accumulation_logic: Each campaign = one scored iteration. Tier recalculated after every new iteration using rolling average. Single bad iteration does not reset Gold status. Minimum 2 iterations required before Gold can be assigned.

tiers.label: Gold
tiers.description: Ambassador potential. Consistent quality, high qCPE, long-term content value.
tiers.next_action: Propose ambassador program. Priority invite to all future campaigns.

compensation_model.performance_bonus: % of bookings made with personal promo code (hybrid model)
compensation_model.rationale: Research confirms hybrid base + KPI bonus is market direction. Financial stake maintains long-term engagement. Discount up to 38% on CAC with annual contracts.

tiers.label: Silver
tiers.description: Good output, reusable content, room to grow.
tiers.next_action: Invite to next campaign cycle. Consider upgrading deliverable expectations.

compensation_model.rationale: Standard gifting model. Proven ROI entry format.

tiers.label: Out
tiers.description: Low content quality, poor engagement, or misaligned audience.
tiers.next_action: Archive in Attio. No further outreach. Document reason.

project_builder.description: Generates personalised story frames per influencer. Replaces generic briefs with narrative-first emotional prompts. Grounded in self-disclosure and parasocial psychology.
project_builder.principle: We reveal the influencer's individuality through our product — not the other way around. Frames are questions, not instructions.

adventurous: Imagine your partner surprised you with a night in the middle of nowhere — no city noise, just stars and fire. How would you feel in that moment?
What would you do if you had 48 hours completely to yourselves — no plans, no agenda?
If you could design the perfect romantic escape for two, what would it look like?
romantic: Think about the last time you felt completely present with your partner. What made it possible?
What does it feel like when the world disappears and it's just the two of you?
If love had a place — a physical space that matched how you feel — what would it look like?
aesthetic: What does the perfect light for a romantic moment look like to you?
If you were to capture intimacy in one image, what scene comes to mind?
Walk us through the aesthetic of your ideal couple's retreat.
default: How would you react if someone organized a completely private escape for you and your partner?
What's the most romantic thing a place has ever made you feel?
Describe your ideal 'disappear from the world' moment with your person.

adventurous: What would your ideal 'disappear and celebrate' birthday look like?
If your closest people could take over one day for you — what would they plan?
What's the birthday experience you've never had but always wanted?
social: How do you like to celebrate — big energy or intimate?
What's the vibe of your perfect birthday — who's there, what's happening?
Describe the best birthday surprise you could imagine.
default: If you could celebrate your birthday anywhere, with no limitations — what would it look like?
What does a meaningful birthday feel like to you, beyond the party?
How would you feel if your people pulled off a completely private celebration just for you?
What does a team feel like when they're actually connecting — not just working?
What's missing from most corporate retreats that makes them feel forgettable?
If you could design one day that would change how your team sees each other, what would happen?

family: What's the trip that would make your kids talk about it for years?
How do you find moments of real connection with your family in the noise of everyday?
If you could give your family one unforgettable memory this year — what would it be?
default: What does quality family time actually look like when everything slows down?
Describe the perfect off-grid family adventure — what's happening, who's there?
What makes a place feel safe and magical for kids and adults at the same time?

wellness: What does it feel like when you finally stop and breathe — really breathe?
Describe your perfect reset. What does it look and feel like?
If rest had a place, what would that place be?
default: When was the last time you felt fully restored? What made that possible?
What does your mind and body need when they've had enough?
Design your ideal wellness retreat — what's essential, what's absent?
What does the perfect wedding backdrop look like to you?
How do you imagine the transition from the ceremony to the celebration?
What feeling do you want guests to carry with them after the day ends?
What environment makes your best thinking happen?
What does a productive strategy session feel like when everything clicks?
How does space affect the quality of decisions your team makes?

adventurous: What does it feel like to compete somewhere unexpected — away from courts and crowds?
Describe your ideal sports weekend — where, who, what energy?
What makes a sports experience memorable beyond the game itself?
default: What would it feel like to play at a private court with nothing around you but nature?
How does the environment change the way you play and connect?
Describe your ultimate outdoor sports escape.

ftc_disclosure.rule: All sponsored content and gifted stays must be disclosed. Federal FTC requirement — applies in all US states.
ftc_disclosure.sc_specific: South Carolina has no additional state-level influencer regulations beyond federal FTC standards. No extra compliance burden.
ftc_disclosure.where_to_add: Onboarding brief — mandatory checklist item before content is published.
ftc_disclosure.manager_action: Confirm disclosure presence before approving content in Review stage.

minors.note: SC follows standard FTC rules. California AB 1880 / SB 764 (minor income protection) does not apply unless filming in CA. No action required for Patrick SC operations.

candidate_examples.note: Real profiles from kone.vc — used to illustrate scoring model. Not an endorsement or confirmed partnership.

zone_a_candidates.name: South Carolina & Destination Photographer (Lina)
zone_a_candidates.notes: Strong couples + romantic content focus. SC-based. High relevance for Romance / Wedding scenarios. ER solid for the follower count. Film aesthetic aligns with Horizons visual identity.

zone_a_candidates.name: Caleb Nealey
zone_a_candidates.notes: Charlotte-based with solid following and events background. Lower keyword relevance to glamping/nature but lifestyle crossover. Could work for social/celebration scenarios. Worth outreach.

zone_b_candidates.name: Explore Athens
zone_b_candidates.notes: Outstanding ER (4.38%) for the size. Community-driven storytelling mirrors Visit Salt Lake approach. Georgia audience — SE regional reach. Adventure / outdoor focus strong fit.

zone_b_candidates.name: Murfreesboro.com
zone_b_candidates.content_focus: local Tennessee community, family activities, travel
zone_b_candidates.notes: Community platform with good reach. Family and local focus. Could work for Family or Corporate scenarios targeting TN audience. ER acceptable. Low glamping keyword density.

watch_list.name: Knoxville Moms
watch_list.notes: Strong glamping keyword hit and family focus but ER is very low (0.42%). Reaches 20k but audience is disengaged. Watch list — not priority outreach.

stages.label: Candidate
checklist: Verify follower count and ER
Check geo zone (A / B / C)
Review content quality and aesthetic fit
Confirm no brand conflicts
Add to Attio as Candidate

stages.label: Outreach
checklist: Send initial DM / email using script
Follow up if no reply in 3 days
Log outreach date in Attio
Qualify: confirm interest and content capacity

stages.label: Onboarding
checklist: Send onboarding brief with story frames
Confirm deliverables and timeline
Set up personalised promo code (e.g. NAME15)
Confirm stay dates if applicable
Add FTC disclosure requirement to brief
Update Attio to Active
stages.ftc_note: Disclosure confirmation is mandatory before publishing. Add as checklist item in brief.

stages.label: In production
checklist: Check in at day 3 of stay
Send midpoint encouragement message
Confirm content schedule and formats
Monitor early story posts if any

stages.label: Review
checklist: Collect all content assets
Review against deliverables checklist
Verify FTC disclosure present on all posts
Count saves, meaningful comments, link clicks (qCPE)
Log content quality score in panel
Flag reusable assets for long-term library

stages.label: Complete
checklist: Score iteration across all 4 axes (quality / value / longevity / qCPE)
Update tier in Attio
Send thank you + next steps message
Archive content with scenario and personality tags
Decide: Gold / Silver / Out

attio_schema.sync_method: Zapier (panel → Attio). Trigger: iteration saved or stage advanced.

scripts.note: Script content to be authored separately in the existing scripts panel (3rd scenario: Influencers). These are structural stubs only.

key_beats: Personal hook — reference their specific content style (not just follower count)
One-line brand intro — Horizons, Patrick SC, private glamping, 80min from Charlotte
Soft offer — complimentary stay, no hard ask yet
Simple CTA — are you open to hearing more?
Keep it under 4 lines — parasocial DMs feel casual, not corporate

onboarding.goal: Brief the influencer without killing their creativity
key_beats: Welcome + genuine excitement framing
Scenario context — what we're highlighting (e.g. couples escape, birthday)
Story frames — send as inspiration, explicitly NOT as a script
Deliverables and timeline
Personal promo code (NAME15 format)
FTC disclosure requirement — clear, non-intimidating
Point of contact and check-in schedule

midpoint_checkin.goal: Maintain energy, unlock more authentic content moments
key_beats: Casual tone — not a status update request
Ask how they're feeling, not what they've posted
Encourage spontaneous moments over planned shots
Remind of any remaining deliverables naturally, not formally

closing.goal: Close the loop and set up next cycle
key_beats: Thank you — genuine, reference a specific content moment
Share a result if available (promo code bookings, saves count)
Tease next collaboration if Silver / Gold
For Gold: soft intro to ambassador concept and hybrid comp model
